context.ocr: Three steps to unfreeze. Online lenders have no way to deal with you. Three steps. Then your WeChat account gets frozen. Quickly go check your freeze page first. context.asr: I received a court summons and a payment order, and then my WeChat account was frozen. Bro, let me teach you three tricks to nullify it. Freezing your account is actually a paper tiger - it won't bite at all. Whether it's WeChat or Alipay that's frozen, quickly go check your freeze page first. Is it property preservation or litigation preservation? For property preservation, it's only pre-litigation preservation for 10 to 30 days. If they don't take follow-up action and don't continue to sue you, it will automatically unfreeze. The platform is actually bluffing at this point, trying to scare you - don't back down. If your freeze page shows litigation preservation, it means you've truly been subjected to litigation proceedings. This type of freeze typically starts at one year, but don't panic with me here. After the freeze, within 15 days, quickly write something called a jurisdictional objection application and mail it to the court that froze your account. Ten days later, they'll unfreeze it for you. Third, the payment order - the court sends you a payment order to force you to repay. Bro, you only need to do one thing: directly challenge it by handwriting a review petition and sending it back to the court, saying they're charging illegal fees and the interest exceeds the limit. Within three days, the payment order will automatically become invalid. Drag it out until their platform collapses on its own. Remember this, bro. 90% of online loan companies suing you are actually just testing whether you understand the law. Follow these three steps I've told you, and no one can do anything to you, bro.

context.asr: This is a thrilling scene captured on surveillance camera. After 9 PM, a pregnant woman was sitting alone on the living room sofa playing with her phone. However, what happened next was unbelievable. For some unknown reason, the cat suddenly rushed rapidly toward the mirror and crashed into it, causing the mirror to wobble precariously and threatening to fall at any moment. The woman nearby saw this and hurriedly rushed over, just in time to catch the falling mirror. Due to the woman's overly vigorous movement, a sharp pain struck her belly, and she squatted on the ground clutching her stomach, continuously calling out for her husband. Meanwhile, the cat was running around frantically like it had gone mad. Upon hearing his wife's cries, the man immediately ran out to check the situation. Seeing his wife squatting on the ground, he rushed over right away, comforting her while asking what happened. After learning what had occurred, the man was deeply shaken and relieved that his wife was alright. So what was wrong with this cat? Why would a perfectly fine cat go and crash into a mirror?
